markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Drift/staleness tracking for compiled wiki pages - the native analog of codewiki's commit-vs-source_files drift check and the S9.1.1 guard that keeps 'query the wiki, not the raw' safe. Every compiled page is stamped with the EventLedger source-version and the exact cited-source set (entity/span/symbol ids + content hashes) it compiled from. A drift checker diffs current authority against stamps and flags exactly which pages are stale and why. Serving a wiki page attaches a staleness verdict fail-closed (reuse the MT-106 StalenessVerdict pattern); the Notes UI receives a machine-readable stale badge contract. Without this MT, MT-241's wiki silently goes stale; they ship together.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Stamps live in PostgreSQL keyed to EventLedger versions; no wall-clock-based staleness heuristics.
Serving a stale page without its verdict is a contract violation (fail-closed).
No false-stale: unchanged sources must not flag pages (negative test required).
scope.expected_behavior: Changing any cited source flags exactly the wiki pages that cited it as STALE with a concrete reason (which source, which version delta); unchanged pages stay FRESH; every served page carries its verdict.
acceptance_criteria: Page stamps record EventLedger version + full cited-source set with content hashes.
Drift check after a real source edit returns exactly the affected page set (proven set equality in test).
Staleness verdict attached on every page-serve path; never silent.
Negative test: recompile with no source change yields zero stale pages.
verification: Real-PG tests: edit-cited-source -> exact stale set; no-change -> no stale; verdict-on-serve.
handoff_requirements: List files changed, migrations, tests with results.
Document the stale-badge contract (fields, values) for the Notes UI + retrieval.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
